prompt:
Implement Phase 2 (PWA Setup) of the Balance plan. Read docs/PLAN.md and CLAUDE.md for context.

## Scope

Make Balance an installable PWA with offline caching. Scheduled push notifications are deferred to Phase 3 (they need a server). This phase focuses on installability and offline support.

## 1. manifest.json (client/manifest.json)

Create a web app manifest:
- name: 'Balance'
- short_name: 'Balance'
- description: 'Personal health and wellness tracker'
- start_url: '/'
- display: 'standalone'
- background_color: '#FAF6F0' (the cream color from the app's theme)
- theme_color: '#4A6741' (the forest/sage color)
- icons: generate simple placeholder icons (use a solid colored square with 'B' letter). Create SVG icons at client/public/icon-192.svg and client/public/icon-512.svg. Reference PNG versions in manifest for compatibility but use SVG as primary.
  - Actually, just create simple PNG placeholder icons using an inline SVG converted approach. The manifest needs:
    - 192x192 icon
    - 512x512 icon
  - For now, create the manifest referencing icon paths and add a TODO comment that real icons are needed. Use the SVG format which is supported in modern Android Chrome.

## 2. Service Worker (client/src/sw.ts)

Create a service worker that:
- Caches the app shell (HTML, CSS, JS) on install using a cache-first strategy
- Uses a versioned cache name so updates clear old caches
- Handles fetch events: serve from cache first, fall back to network
- On activate, clean up old caches
- Keep it simple — no push notification handling yet

## 3. Register Service Worker

In client/src/main.tsx, register the service worker after the app mounts:
- Only register in production (check for localhost)
- Actually, register always so we can test — but use a simple check
- Register with: navigator.serviceWorker.register('/sw.js')

## 4. Vite Configuration

The service worker needs special handling in Vite:
- The SW file should NOT be bundled into the main app bundle
- Use vite-plugin-pwa OR manually configure the SW build
- Simplest approach: use the vite-plugin-pwa package (npm install -D vite-plugin-pwa) which handles SW generation, manifest injection, and caching automatically
- Configure it with the manifest details and caching strategy (cache first for assets)

Actually, vite-plugin-pwa with generateSW mode is the simplest path. It:
- Auto-generates the service worker with Workbox
- Injects the manifest link into HTML
- Handles precaching of build assets
- No manual SW file needed

Use this approach:
- npm install -D vite-plugin-pwa
- Configure VitePWA plugin in vite.config.ts with manifest settings and workbox config
- Remove the need for a manual sw.ts file — let Workbox generate it

## 5. Update index.html

- Add meta viewport tag if missing (for mobile)
- Add theme-color meta tag
- The vite-plugin-pwa will auto-inject the manifest link

## 6. Icons

Create simple SVG icons:
- client/public/icon-192x192.png — For now, create a simple SVG file at client/public/icon.svg (a rounded square in the sage/forest green color with a 'B'). The vite-plugin-pwa can reference SVGs.
- Actually, modern Android Chrome supports SVG maskable icons. Create:
  - client/public/icon.svg — main app icon (sage green rounded rect with white 'B')
  - Reference it in manifest with purpose: 'any maskable'

## 7. Verification

- npm run lint and npm run build must pass
- The build output should include a manifest.webmanifest and a sw.js file
- Check that the manifest is properly configured

Create PR titled 'Phase 2: PWA — installable app with offline caching'
